name: Department of Commerce
description_short: Department of Commerce Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union offering checking, savings, lending, and credit card services to all U.S. citizens and resident aliens.
description_long:
Department of Commerce Federal Credit Union (DOCFCU) is a federally-chartered credit union operating under NCUA insurance with routing number 254074439. Historically serving Department of Commerce employees, the institution recently expanded membership to all U.S. citizens and resident aliens, broadening its member base significantly. The credit union operates a full-service financial institution with both physical branches and digital banking capabilities through their website and mobile app.

DOCFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including interest-bearing checking accounts (Performance Checking with up to 3.50% APY), share certificates (CDs) with rates up to 2.12% APY, auto loans starting at 5.74% APR, home equity lines of credit (up to $350,000 with no closing costs), mortgage services, credit cards with rates as low as 12.99% APR and no annual fees, and specialized loan products like 1040 Tax Loans. The institution emphasizes digital convenience through bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and integration with financial management tools like Quicken and TurboTax.

DOCFCU distinguishes itself through competitive rates on deposit products, flexible lending options with online approval processes, transparent fee structures (no annual credit card fees, no checking account minimums), and member testimonials highlighting superior service during refinancing and debt consolidation. The credit union actively communicates member benefits through monthly newsletters and seasonal promotional campaigns, and maintains strong fraud awareness with scam alerts on their homepage.

As a credit union, DOCFCU operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model, meaning earnings are returned to members rather than external shareholders. However, the website provides limited transparency regarding membership eligibility requirements beyond the recent expansion announcement, specific APR ranges for various loan products, and detailed fee schedules. Member service quality appears solid based on testimonials, though geographic service limitations may apply depending on branch locations.
services: Performance Checking accounts with interest dividends and no minimum balance
Share Certificates (CDs) with terms from 6 months to 5 years, minimum $500 deposits
Auto loans with competitive rates and online approval
Home equity lines of credit and fixed-rate equity loans up to $350,000
Mortgage services including home purchase and refinancing
Credit cards with low rates and no annual fees
VISA balance transfer cards with free transfer service
1040 Tax Loans with flexible terms up to 1040 days
Mobile banking app with bill pay, remote deposit, and e-statements
Money management tools powered by Quicken integration
Vehicle buying service for auto purchases
Online account enrollment and digital member services
pros: Interest-bearing checking account with no minimum balance and no monthly maintenance fees
Competitive rates on deposit products (3.50% APY checking, 2.12% APY share certificates)
Auto loans available with online approval and rates as low as 5.74% APR
Home equity lines of credit up to $350,000 with no closing costs
Credit cards with zero annual fees and no cash advance fees
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure ensures earnings benefit members
Comprehensive mobile app and digital banking services including remote deposit and bill pay
cons: Website lacks detailed fee schedules and full APR range information for all loan products
Geographic service limitations not clearly specified; may require branch proximity for full services
Limited transparency on membership eligibility criteria despite recent expansion to all U.S. citizens
Promotional rates (1040 Tax Loan at 10.40% APR) are time-limited and not standard offerings
best_for: Federal employees and Department of Commerce workers seeking member-focused financial services
Consumers wanting interest-bearing checking accounts without minimums or maintenance fees
Borrowers seeking competitive auto loan rates and home equity financing with transparent terms
Members prioritizing digital banking convenience with mobile app and online account management
diagnosis: DOCFCU is ideal for U.S. citizens seeking a member-owned credit union with competitive rates on both deposits and lending, particularly those wanting no-fee checking with interest earnings and streamlined digital banking. The main caveat is limited transparency on fee schedules and membership service areas, requiring direct contact to confirm eligibility and availability of specific products in your geographic location.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Department of Commerce — NCUA-insured credit union in Washington, DC. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
